name: Apis Partners LLP
description: Apis Partners is a private equity asset manager focused on financial services in Africa and Asia. Apis Partners core investment team comprises of 14 investment professionals based in London and Lagos with specialised expertise in financial services in growth markets. Apis Partners operating network includes on-the-ground presence in 5 countries and over 20 additional financial services sub-sector experts. Apis Partners is the manager of Apis Growth Fund I, which has closed over US$ 287 million in commitments from leading international investors. Apis Growth Fund I provides catalytic growth equity capital to financial services companies in Africa and South Asia, namely those that are well positioned to benefit from favourable demographic and economic trends, strong structural supply/demand gaps and high financial services growth.

* Apis Partners collects $1.23 bn for new private equity fund, exceeds target
# Apis Partners collects $1.23 bn for new private equity fund, exceeds target
By [Malvika Maloo](https://www.vccircle.com/author/malvika-maloo)
* 07 May 2026
* **Listen to Story**
* [](https://web.whatsapp.com/send?text=https://www.vccircle.com/apispartners-collects-1-23-bn-for-new-private-equity-fund)
* [__](http://www.facebook.com/sharer.php?u=https://www.vccircle.com/apispartners-collects-1-23-bn-for-new-private-equity-fund&p\[title\]=Apis+Partners+collects+$1.23+bn+for+new+private+equity+fund,+exceeds+target)
* [](https://twitter.com/share?url=https://www.vccircle.com/apispartners-collects-1-23-bn-for-new-private-equity-fund&via=vccircle&related=twitterapi%2Ctwitter&%20hashtags=VCCircle&text=Apis+Partners+collects+123+bn+for+new+private+equity+fund+exceeds+target)
* [__](http://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.vccircle.com/apispartners-collects-1-23-bn-for-new-private-equity-fund&title=Apis+Partners+collects+$1.23+bn+for+new+private+equity+fund,+exceeds+target&source=vccircle.com)
![Apis Partners collects $1.23 bn for new private equity fund, exceeds target](https://www.vccircle.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fassets.vccircle.com%2Fuploads%2F2024%2F03%2FApis-Partners.jpg&w=3840&q=75)
Matteo Stefanel (left) and Udayan Goyal, managing partners, Apis
Apis Partners, a London-headquartered private equity firm that counts edtech startup Leap, insure-tech company RenewBuy and non-bank lender Hero Fincorp in its India portfolio, has closed its third fund after collecting double the corpus of its predecessor.
The PE firm raised $1.23 billion for Apis Global Growth Fund III and Apis Growth Markets Fund III, excluding co-investment commitments, it said in a statement.
The two vehicles, collectively its third fund, closed 23% above the PE firm’s initial target. About 70% of its existing [limited partners](https://www.vccircle.com/tag/limited-partners) committed to half of the corpus, Apis said.
Advertisement
The PE firm’s assets under management now stand at $2.3 billion.
The new fund will invest in tech-enabled financial infrastructure and services companies across Europe and growth markets. Its focus is on high growth, profitable financial infrastructure and services businesses in these regions.
“We continue to see opportunities in our target segment of profitable, high growth, tech-enabled financial infrastructure and services companies,” said Matteo Stefanel, Apis co-founder and managing partner. “We are well on our way to replicating the success of previous funds, by leveraging our experience in this sector and bringing capital and connectivity to our portfolio companies as we invest in the conduits to global economic activity, the pipes of the finance industry.”
Advertisement
The new fund has already invested $400 million across seven companies. These include the UK’s digital wealth management firm MoneyBox, European and Asian platform for prepaid digital goods Coda Recharge, and digital payments infrastructure provider Thunes.
“We look forward to continuing to invest in our key themes of embedded finance, democratisation of finance and the deepening of the digital economy through capital-light, high growth disruptive business models around the world whilst applying our proprietary impact methodology,” said Udayan Goyal, Apis co-founder and managing partner.
The PE firm[ hit the road for the third fund](https://www.vccircle.com/apispartners-new-fund-to-sign-bigger-cheques-returning-lp-may-put-over-2x) in early 2023. It[ received commitments](https://www.vccircle.com/apis-partners-raises-550-mn-for-second-pe-fund-exceeds-target) worth $563 million for its second vehicle that closed in 2019, after exceeding the target of $400 million.
Advertisement
[Apis Partners](https://www.vccircle.com/tag/apis-partners) said its funds have generated in excess of $1 billion in realisations to date, aided by recent exits from iKhokha and Baobab to Nedbank and Beltone Capital respectively.

tavily.answer:
Apis Partners' official website is apis.pe. The company is based in London, England, and also has offices in Singapore and Dubai.
Apis Partners is a private equity firm focused on financial services, headquartered in London, with over 40 employees. It invests in financial infrastructure and services, aiming for financial inclusion. Founded in 2014, it has US$2.3 billion in assets under management.
Apis Partners is a private equity firm focused on financial services, founded in 2014 with $2.3 billion in assets under management. It invests in tech-enabled financial infrastructure across Europe and growth markets. The firm's Crunchbase page does not provide specific funding details.
Apis Partners is a private equity firm focused on financial infrastructure and services, headquartered in London, managing over $2 billion in committed capital. It has raised $1.23 billion for its third fund, exceeding its target. Apis Partners invests in tech-enabled financial businesses with a focus on long-term growth and value creation.
Apis Partners is a private equity firm investing in financial technology and services, recently closed a $1.23 billion fund, and has invested in companies like BIPO and Paymentology.

target_market: high growth, profitable financial infrastructure and services businesses in Europe and growth markets
